method: source-backed intake file; replace or append with live exports from YouTube, TikTok Creative Center, Google Trends, Reddit, or LinkedIn.

topCandidate.topic: AI resumes all sound the same, so recruiters search for proof
topCandidate.hook: Your AI resume sounds professional. That might be the problem.
topCandidate.whyNow: AI-written resumes are common enough that job seekers need a trust-building way to show real proof, not generic polish.
topCandidate.contentAngle: Recruiter-style teardown of an AI-polished resume that misses HubSpot, CAC, LinkedIn Ads, and lifecycle marketing.

sourceNotes.title: YouTube Analytics engagement and retention
sourceNotes.note: Monitor retention and engagement after posting, not just views.

sourceNotes.title: TikTok Creative Center
sourceNotes.note: Use trend discovery to validate current hooks and sounds before publishing.

candidates.topic: The ATS auto-reject myth versus recruiter search reality
candidates.hook: The ATS probably did not throw your resume away. Here is the real problem.
candidates.whyNow: Fear-based ATS content gets clicks, but a clearer explanation builds more trust and conversion.
candidates.contentAngle: Show the difference between parsing/indexing/search and unsupported auto-reject claims.

sourceNotes.title: Signal viral content workflow
sourceNotes.note: Keep claims safe and avoid adversarial ATS promises.
